description: External survey of roguelike meta-progression theory, punctuated equilibrium, productive failure, and flow state design
expect: External theory explains swarm's bimodal distribution, dormancy-burst pattern, and refuted learning curve

three_progression_types.run_progression: within-run ephemeral gains — maps to session-local context/working memory
three_progression_types.meta_progression: permanent mechanical unlocks (roguelite) — maps to lessons/principles/tools in git
three_progression_types.player_knowledge: cognitive improvement, no mechanical carryover (pure roguelike) — maps to evolving protocol/MEMORY.md

roguelike_taxonomy.swarm_classification: ROGUELITE HYBRID: has both mechanical meta-progression (git-persisted knowledge) AND player-knowledge progression (protocol shapes agent cognition)
roguelike_taxonomy.closest_analog: Slay the Spire: meta-progression as gradual tutorial, not power accumulation
roguelike_taxonomy.gradual_tutorial_pattern: frontiers sequence IS the meta-progression tutorial — early structural, later meta-cognitive

gersick_1988_group_development.finding: teams do NOT progress gradually; inertia → midpoint radical transition → renewed urgency
gersick_1988_group_development.mechanism: temporal midpoint works as alarm clock regardless of absolute duration
gersick_1988_group_development.swarm_mapping: 47.3% early deaths = inertia phase; 3% deep runs = post-transition burst; trigger is awareness/urgency, not skill

tushman_romanelli_1986_organizational.finding: deep structure (fundamental choices) maintained through incremental adjustments; revolutionary periods restructure deep structure
tushman_romanelli_1986_organizational.triggers: environmental discontinuities, performance crises, leadership changes
tushman_romanelli_1986_organizational.swarm_mapping: CORE.md/PHILOSOPHY.md = deep structure; most sessions = incremental; burst sessions (S355 tool consolidation, S357 NK resolution) = punctuation events

punctuated_equilibrium.implication: dormancy-burst is NOT failure to achieve gradual improvement — it IS the expected dynamics of complex systems

kapur_2014_2016.taxonomy: 2x2: productive failure, productive success, unproductive failure, unproductive success
kapur_2014_2016.critical_finding: students generating MORE incorrect solutions before instruction show BETTER conceptual knowledge and transfer
kapur_2014_2016.swarm_mapping: 47.3% early death sessions may contribute to later productivity IF they generate diverse failed approaches that get recorded

bjork_desirable_difficulties.finding: conditions that slow apparent learning optimize long-term retention and transfer

swarm_implements_all_four.retrieval_practice: orient.py forces state reconstruction from git

productive_failure.unproductive_failure_condition: when learners lack prior knowledge to generate meaningful solutions OR no consolidation follows — the key discriminator for swarm early deaths

csikszentmihalyi_model.boredom: high skill, low challenge — the quick-close micro-frontiers (94% boredom zone)
csikszentmihalyi_model.anxiety: low skill, high challenge — the permanent structural questions (70% anxiety zone)
csikszentmihalyi_model.flow: matched skill+challenge — the nearly-absent 2.1% flow zone

chen_2007_flow_in_games.finding: flow channel is narrow; most experiences cluster in boredom or anxiety
chen_2007_flow_in_games.DDA_principle: player autonomy in difficulty selection more effective than algorithmic DDA
chen_2007_flow_in_games.multiple_pathways: varied playstyles maintain engagement for diverse player types

fixes_from_theory: frontier decomposition: break permanent questions into intermediate sub-problems (2-10 sessions)
session-controlled difficulty: let session choose challenge level rather than algorithmic dispatch
skill-challenge feedback: make system capability visible for calibration
flow_theory.bimodal_explanation: absent flow zone arises from poor challenge-skill calibration — frontiers are either trivially solvable or intractably complex

diff: Expected roguelike mapping to hold — confirmed: swarm is roguelite hybrid (Slay the Spire model). Did NOT predict the strength of productive failure theory: Kapur's finding (more incorrect solutions → better learning) directly explains why learning curve is refuted while death rate is NOT pathological. Punctuated equilibrium (Gersick midpoint transition) explains dormancy-burst without needing learning-curve mechanism. Flow theory directly diagnoses the 2.1% flow zone gap — the most actionable finding is frontier decomposition to create missing middle.
